Who We Serve

Built for Families.
Trusted by Coordinators.

Open Grace LLC exists to serve two audiences who share the same goal — a better quality of life for individuals with developmental disabilities.

🏠
For Families
Your family deserves a dedicated partner
We show up, listen, and work alongside your family to navigate resources, build skills, and create a stronger future — at a pace that feels right for you.
  • Personalized support tailored to your loved one's goals
  • Help navigating benefits, agencies, and systems
  • Building independence and life skills over time
  • Connecting you to community resources and programs
  • Available in English and Spanish
  • No rush — we work at your family's pace
Ready to learn more? Call or text us at (951) 880-4132 — no obligation, just a conversation.
👔
For Service Coordinators
A CFS partner you can trust with your families
Open Grace LLC is a vendorized CFS provider under IRC Vendor PJ6208. We serve families in Riverside and San Bernardino counties with professional, compliant, and compassionate care.
  • IPP-aligned service delivery and documentation
  • IRC-compliant daily contact logs and progress notes
  • Bilingual capability — English and Spanish
  • Mandated reporter trained staff
  • Responsive to coordinator communication
  • Currently accepting new referrals
Submit a referral or ask a question Contact Director Joshua Kennedy at (951) 880-4132 or info@opengrace.org
The Process

How Connection Happens

Getting started with Open Grace is simple. Your IRC Service Coordinator initiates the process and we handle the rest.

1
💬

IRC Referral Is Made

Your IRC Service Coordinator identifies Open Grace LLC as a CFS provider and submits a referral through the Regional Center. Families can also request a referral at their next IPP meeting.

2
🤝

Personal Introduction

Director Joshua Kennedy personally reaches out to your family — by phone or text — to introduce Open Grace, answer questions, and schedule a no-pressure intake conversation at your convenience.

3
🌟

Services Begin

Once authorized through your IPP, your dedicated Family Coordinator begins working alongside your family — building trust, navigating systems, and providing support tailored to your goals.

What We Provide

8 Areas of Support

CFS is flexible and individualized. Most families have multiple needs at the same time — Open Grace addresses them holistically.

📋
Benefits & System Navigation
SSI, IHSS, Medi-Cal, CalFresh applications, renewals, and advocacy with agencies.
🚐
Transportation Support
Finding, applying for, and learning to use transportation services independently.
📈
Long-Term Planning
Conservatorship, future housing, Special Needs Trust, and aging caregiver planning.
📋
Care Coordination
Managing appointments and multiple providers so nothing falls through the cracks.
🏠
Independent Living Skills
Daily routines, hygiene, money management, and step-by-step independence building.
❤
Caregiver Support & Respite
Respite services, backup caregiving plans, and reducing caregiver burnout.
💬
Behavioral & Communication
Behavioral health referrals, AAC tools, caregiver training, and specialist coordination.
🌏
Community & Employment
DOR referrals, job readiness, social skills, recreational activities, and community engagement.

For Service Coordinators

Referring a Family to Open Grace

We make the referral process simple and we follow up promptly with every family you send our way.

1

Identify the family's CFS needs

Review the family's IPP and confirm CFS authorization. Open Grace accepts referrals for benefits navigation, care coordination, independent living, long-term planning, and all other CFS service areas.

2

Contact Director Joshua Kennedy directly

Call or email Joshua to discuss the referral, confirm availability, and share any background that would help us serve the family well from day one.

3

We reach out to the family within 48 hours

Joshua personally contacts the family to introduce Open Grace, answer questions, and schedule an intake conversation — no pressure, no rush.

4

Services begin and documentation follows

Once authorized, services begin immediately. You will receive regular progress updates and all documentation is maintained in full IRC compliance.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything families and service coordinators need to know about CFS services through Open Grace.

1

What is Coordinated Family Support (CFS)?

▼

CFS is a Regional Center service that provides individualized support to adults with developmental disabilities who live at home with their family. The program focuses on increasing independence, supporting caregivers, and connecting families to the services and resources they need. Services are flexible and tailored to each family's unique situation and IPP goals.

2

Who qualifies for CFS services?

▼

To receive CFS services the individual must:

  • Be 18 years of age or older
  • Have a developmental disability
  • Live at home with family or caregivers
  • Be eligible through a Regional Center such as Inland Counties Regional Center (IRC)
  • Have CFS authorized in their current Individual Program Plan (IPP)
3

Does CFS replace services like IHSS or ABA?

▼

No. CFS does not replace or duplicate any existing services. Instead CFS helps your family get approved for services you may not have yet, ensures your existing services are working together effectively, and fills the gaps that other services do not cover. Think of Open Grace as the coordinator that brings everything together for your family.

4

What are the most common reasons families use CFS?

▼

Based on referrals received through IRC the most common reasons families seek CFS services include:

  • Help applying for or maintaining benefits like SSI, IHSS, and Medi-Cal
  • Finding and setting up reliable transportation
  • Long-term planning — what happens when caregivers age
  • Managing multiple providers and appointments
  • Building daily living and independence skills
  • Caregiver burnout and need for respite support
  • Communication tools for non-verbal individuals
5

Can Open Grace support families who primarily speak Spanish?

▼

Yes. Open Grace provides culturally appropriate, family-centered services and can support families who primarily speak Spanish. We can facilitate communication with providers and agencies to ensure your family fully understands the services and supports available to you.

6

Can Open Grace come to appointments or agency visits with us?

▼

Yes. When appropriate Open Grace Family Coordinators may support your family during appointments by helping explain information, advocating for your loved one, and ensuring your concerns are heard and addressed by the agency or provider.

7

Can CFS help with non-verbal individuals or complex needs?

▼

Yes. Open Grace can help obtain communication tools such as AAC devices and PECS, coordinate specialized services, train caregivers and providers, and provide support tailored to individuals with complex medical or behavioral needs. Every individual deserves support that meets them where they are.

8

How do I get started?

▼

Getting started is simple. Ask your IRC Service Coordinator about a referral to Open Grace LLC for CFS services. You can also contact Director Joshua Kennedy directly at (951) 880-4132 or info@opengrace.org. Once a referral is made Joshua will personally reach out to your family to introduce Open Grace and answer any questions — no pressure, no rush.

9

What makes Open Grace different?

▼

Open Grace takes a whole-family approach. We do not just check a box — we show up, listen, and work alongside your family to navigate complex systems. Director Joshua Kennedy personally connects with every family we serve. We are not just a service provider — we are a partner, a navigator, and a stabilizer for families who need someone in their corner.

Ready to Connect?

No rush and no pressure. Reach out whenever your family is ready — or contact us to discuss a referral.
